mnemonics.title: Access Specifiers Order
mnemonics.content: P-P-P: Private is Primary, Protected is Partial, Public is Permitted - remembering the hierarchy of restriction from most restricted to least.

mnemonics.title: Constructor vs Destructor
mnemonics.content: CONSTRUCT: C-reate, O-bject, N-ew, S-tate; DESTRUCT: D-elete, E-liminate, S-tate, T-erminate - both manage object lifecycle.

mnemonics.title: This Pointer Purpose
mnemonics.content: THIS points to THIS object - the implicit pointer always refers to the current instance.

acronyms.usage: Paradigm focusing on objects rather than procedures
The name indicates it's an extension of C language
Types: Default, Parameterized, Copy, and Dynamic memory constructors
Rule: Public members form the interface, private members provide encapsulation

visualTips.visualization: Think of a class as an architectural blueprint - a house plan (class) vs actual houses (objects) built from it.
Visualize as layers: private at core (protected), surrounded by derived classes (protected), outer layer accessible to all (public).
Each object gets its own copy of non-static data members; member functions are shared across all objects.

analogies.analogy: Class is like a recipe card; objects are the actual dishes cooked using that recipe.
Like a capsule containing medicine - data (medicine) is protected inside, with controlled access through interface (capsule coating).
Like 'myself' in English - when you say 'I am happy', 'I' refers to yourself; 'this' refers to the current object.
Like a factory setting up a new product line - preparing and initializing everything before the product goes live.
